modifierDescriptions.corruption: Affects Bluff checks to get away with crimes, Stealth checks to hide in crowds. Alignment: Evil +1
modifierDescriptions.crime: Affects Sense Motive to avoid being bluffed, Sleight of Hand to pick pockets. Alignment: Chaotic +1
modifierDescriptions.economy: Affects Craft, Perform, and Profession checks to generate income. Not affected by alignment
modifierDescriptions.law: Affects Intimidate to force compliance, Diplomacy to negotiate with officials. Alignment: Lawful +1
modifierDescriptions.lore: Affects Diplomacy to gather info, Knowledge checks in local libraries. Alignment: Neutral +1 per neutral axis (N = +2)
modifierDescriptions.society: Affects Disguise to blend in, Diplomacy to adjust NPC attitudes. Alignment: Good +1

alignmentModifiers.note: Alignment bonuses are already included in the published modifier totals for each settlement. Theocracy government doubles these.

generalStore.name: General Store
generalStore.description: Standard adventuring supplies and household goods

magicShop.name: Magic Shop
magicShop.description: Enchanted items, scrolls, and arcane curiosities

temple.name: Temple / Shrine
temple.description: Healing, divine scrolls, and holy water

tailor.name: Tailor / Outfitter
tailor.description: Clothing, cloaks, and minor enchanted garments

bookshop.name: Bookshop / Scribe
bookshop.description: Books, maps, spell scrolls, and writing supplies

blackMarket.name: Black Market
blackMarket.description: Rare, illegal, and hard-to-find goods at a premium

sandpoint.name: Sandpoint
sandpoint.title: Light of the Lost Coast
sandpoint.demographics: 1,116 humans, 37 halflings, 25 elves, 24 dwarves, 13 gnomes, 13 half-elves, 12 half-orcs
sandpoint.source: Rise of the Runelords Anniversary Edition; Sandpoint, Light of the Lost Coast
sandpoint.description: A friendly coastal town on the Varisian Gulf, known as the Light of the Lost Coast. Recently rebuilt its cathedral after the Late Unpleasantness.

merchants.name: Savah's Armory
merchants.personality: Fair-dealing warrior woman who keeps a +1 dragon-bane longsword under the counter
merchants.description: The best weapons and armor shop in Sandpoint, stocking a wide variety of martial equipment

merchants.name: Red Dog Smithy
merchants.personality: Foul-tempered, perpetually drunk smith with a pack of red-furred dogs guarding the forge
merchants.description: Sandpoint's working forge; cheaper than Savah's but rougher quality and ruder service

merchants.name: The Feathered Serpent
merchants.personality: Eccentric Vudrani collector of exotic curiosities from across Golarion
merchants.description: Exotic magic items, potions, scrolls, and arcane curiosities

merchants.name: Bottled Solutions
merchants.personality: Nervous, twitchy man who secretly deals in poisons; member of the Sczarni
merchants.description: Alchemical supplies and potions. Discreet customers can acquire poisons.

merchants.name: Ven Vinder's General Store
merchants.personality: Overprotective father of two daughters, gruff but honest merchant
merchants.description: The largest general store in Sandpoint, stocking everything an adventurer needs

merchants.name: Sandpoint Cathedral
merchants.personality: Kind and gentle cleric of Desna who tends to the spiritual needs of the community
merchants.deity: Desna (also serves Erastil, Abadar, Shelyn, Gozreh, Sarenrae)
merchants.description: Grand cathedral dedicated to six deities; primary source of healing services

merchants.name: Sandpoint Jeweler
merchants.personality: Meticulous craftsman who appraises gems and creates fine jewelry
merchants.description: Fine jewelry, gem appraisal, and custom pieces

merchants.name: The Curious Goblin
merchants.personality: Quiet bookworm who knows a little about everything
merchants.description: Books, maps, and rare texts from across Varisia

merchants.name: Goblin Squash Stables
merchants.personality: Gruff retired ranger who decorates his stables with goblin ears
merchants.description: Mounts and riding supplies; boarding at 5 sp/day

magnimar.name: Magnimar
magnimar.title: The City of Monuments
magnimar.demographics: 13,307 humans; 821 halflings; 659 dwarves; 655 elves; 493 gnomes; 166 half-elves; 164 half-orcs; 163 other
magnimar.source: Magnimar, City of Monuments; Inner Sea World Guide
magnimar.description: The City of Monuments, a thriving metropolis at the mouth of the Yondabakari River, dominated by the colossal Irespan bridge ruin.

merchants.name: Bazaar of Sails — Arms Dealers
merchants.personality: Competitive weapon merchants hawk their wares from colorful stalls under billowing canvas
merchants.description: Open-air weapon dealers in the famous Bazaar of Sails

merchants.name: The Golemworks
merchants.personality: Cold, analytical artificer obsessed with construct creation
merchants.description: Magnimar's premier magical workshop — high-end items and custom commissions

merchants.name: The Night Scales
merchants.personality: Magnimar's thieves' guild deals in stolen and forbidden goods through intermediaries in the Shadow district
merchants.description: Underground black market fence network — ask at the right taverns in the Shadow

merchants.name: Temple of Abadar
merchants.personality: The banking temple runs healing services with the precision of financial transactions
merchants.description: Full divine spellcasting services up to 8th level

merchants.name: Bazaar of Sails — General Market
merchants.personality: Bustling open-air market with hundreds of stalls selling goods from across Avistan and Garund
merchants.description: Massive open-air bazaar — if it exists, someone here sells it

merchants.name: Dockway Alchemists
merchants.personality: Several competing alchemist shops along the Dockway, from reputable to questionable
merchants.description: Dockway's alchemist row — best selection in Varisia

merchants.name: Naos Jewelers Row
merchants.personality: Upscale jewelers in the wealthy Naos district cater to nobility and adventurers alike
merchants.description: Fine jewelry, enchanted rings, and precious gems in Magnimar's wealthiest district

turtleback_ferry.name: Turtleback Ferry
turtleback_ferry.source: Rise of the Runelords Anniversary Edition — The Hook Mountain Massacre
turtleback_ferry.description: A remote frontier village on Claybottom Lake, the last stop before Fort Rannick and the ogre-haunted Hook Mountain.

merchants.name: Turtleback General Goods
merchants.personality: Cautious frontier trader who marks up prices due to the remote location
merchants.description: Basic supplies for life on the frontier — limited stock, higher prices

merchants.name: Church of Erastil
merchants.personality: Aged cleric of Erastil who also serves as the village mayor; deeply worried about Fort Rannick's silence
merchants.description: Humble frontier church — limited divine services

merchants.name: Bottoms Up Tavern
merchants.personality: Warm innkeeper who serves as the social hub of the village
merchants.description: Village tavern and inn — cheap rooms and local gossip

kaer_maga.name: Kaer Maga
kaer_maga.title: The City of Strangers
kaer_maga.demographics: 5,500 humans, 500 halflings, 400 dwarves, 400 half-elves, 400 half-orcs, 200 gnomes, 100 elves, 100 orcs, 75 trolls, 50 centaurs, 50 nagas, 175 others
kaer_maga.description: An anarchic city built atop a massive cliff mesa, where anything can be bought and no questions are asked. A frequent resupply point for adventurers heading into the Storval Plateau.

merchants.name: Troll Augurs' Stalls
merchants.personality: Trolls who read the future in their own regenerating entrails — also sell magical curiosities
merchants.description: Magical goods from the famously strange Troll Augurs of the Bis district

merchants.name: Downmarket
merchants.personality: In the Bottoms district, anything is for sale if you have the coin — no questions, no judgment
merchants.description: Kaer Maga's anything-goes underground market

merchants.name: Hospice Market
merchants.personality: Open-air market in the Hospice district where caravans unload goods from across Varisia
merchants.description: Main trading market for caravans crossing the Storval Plateau

merchants.name: Oriat Forges
merchants.personality: Dwarven and half-orc smiths who forge weapons designed for the harsh Storval conditions
merchants.description: Quality arms crafted for the dangers of the Storval Plateau

merchants.name: Ankar-Te Alchemists
merchants.personality: The necromancy-tolerant Ankar-Te district hosts alchemists who push ethical boundaries
merchants.description: Potions and reagents of questionable provenance

riddleport.name: Riddleport
riddleport.title: City of Cyphers
riddleport.demographics: 77% human, 7% dwarf, 5% half-elf, 4% half-orc, 3% tiefling, 2% halfling, 1% gnome, 1% other
riddleport.source: Pathfinder Adventure Path: Second Darkness; Inner Sea World Guide
riddleport.description: A pirate-ruled port city in northern Varisia, dominated by crimelords and the mysterious Cyphergate arch spanning the harbor.

merchants.name: The Publican House
merchants.personality: The most powerful crimelord in Riddleport runs a high-end fence through her pleasure house
merchants.description: High-end black market accessible through the city's crimelords

merchants.name: Riddleport Docks Market
merchants.personality: Rough-and-tumble dock market where sailors and pirates trade plunder and supplies
merchants.description: Dockside market dealing in cargo from across the Inner Sea

merchants.name: Riddleport Forges
merchants.personality: Arms dealers who equip pirates, mercenaries, and the occasional adventurer
merchants.description: Weapons and armor for sea-going fighters

merchants.name: Order of Cyphers
merchants.personality: Scholarly mages who study the Cyphergate offer magical goods and services as a sideline
merchants.description: Arcane goods from the enigmatic Cyphermages

korvosa.name: Korvosa
korvosa.title: The Jewel of Varisia
korvosa.demographics: 90% human, 4% dwarf, 2% elf, 2% halfling, 1% half-elf, 1% other
korvosa.source: Pathfinder Adventure Path: Curse of the Crimson Throne; Guide to Korvosa
korvosa.description: Varisia's oldest and most traditional city, perched on the banks of the Jeggare River. Rivals Magnimar politically and economically.

merchants.name: Gold Market
merchants.personality: Korvosa's tightly regulated main market — all transactions taxed by the crown
merchants.description: The regulated central marketplace of Korvosa

merchants.name: Citadel Armory District
merchants.personality: Arms dealers clustered near Citadel Volshyenek supply the city guard and adventurers
merchants.description: Military-grade arms and armor near the city garrison

merchants.name: Temple of Abadar (Bank of Abadar)
merchants.personality: Korvosa's wealthiest temple doubles as the city's main bank — healing is a financial service
merchants.description: Full divine spellcasting and banking services

merchants.name: Acadamae Annex Shop
merchants.personality: Student-run shop outside the infamous Acadamae sells minor magical goods to fund their studies
merchants.description: Affordable magical goods from aspiring wizards

galduria.name: Galduria
galduria.source: Inner Sea World Guide; Towns of the Inner Sea
galduria.description: A small Varisian town on the western shore of Ember Lake, notable for the Twilight Academy — a school of arcane magic.

merchants.name: Galduria Market
merchants.personality: Quiet town market catering to Academy students and local farmers
merchants.description: Small-town market with basic supplies

merchants.name: Twilight Academy Supply
merchants.personality: The Twilight Academy sells scrolls and minor magical supplies to approved buyers
merchants.description: Academic magic supply shop attached to the Twilight Academy

wolfs_ear.name: Wolf's Ear
wolfs_ear.description: A lumber town with a dark history as a haven for lycanthropes, annexed by Magnimar in 4671 AR. Locals are suspicious of outsiders, especially around the full moon.

merchants.name: Wolf's Ear Trading Post
merchants.personality: Suspicious trader who keeps silver weapons prominently displayed
merchants.description: Frontier trading post with an emphasis on protection against lycanthropes

merchants.name: Wolf's Ear Forge
merchants.personality: Smith who specializes in silver-coated weapons due to local demand
merchants.description: Lumber-town forge that does brisk business in silvered blades

commonServices.name: Raise Dead
commonServices.description: Brings back the dead (5000 gp material)

haggling.failPenalty: Merchant refuses further haggling for 1d4 days

poorRoom.name: Room, poor (common room floor)
poorRoom.description: A spot on the floor of a common room near the fire

commonRoom.name: Room, common
commonRoom.description: A private room with a bed, chamber pot, and candle

goodRoom.name: Room, good
goodRoom.description: A comfortable room with a proper bed, wash basin, desk, and lock

suite.name: Suite
suite.description: A lavish suite with a sitting room, fine bedding, and personal service

stabling.name: Stabling (per mount)
stabling.description: Feed, water, and shelter for one mount

destitute.name: Destitute
destitute.description: Sleep on the streets, beg for scraps. DC 15 Fort save each day or become fatigued

poor.name: Poor
poor.description: Shared housing, simple meals. -2 to Diplomacy and Bluff with middle-class or higher

average.name: Average
average.description: Modest home, decent food, basic comforts

wealthy.name: Wealthy
wealthy.description: Fine home, quality food, servants. +2 circumstance to Diplomacy with merchants

extravagant.name: Extravagant
extravagant.description: Lavish estate, personal chef, retainers. +4 circumstance to Diplomacy with nobility

bath.name: Bath
bath.description: A hot bath at an inn or bathhouse

sandpoint.name: The Rusty Dragon
sandpoint.description: The most popular tavern in Sandpoint. A favorite of adventurers, known for excellent food and a welcoming atmosphere. Ameiko plays samisen music some evenings.
sandpoint.atmosphere: Warm, adventurous, cosmopolitan. Trophy weapons hang from the walls. A stuffed dragon head with a rusted iron nail serves as the sign.
sandpoint.specialFeature: Adventurers who tell a good story of their exploits get 50% off lodging (Perform DC 15 or Diplomacy DC 12)
sandpoint.source: Rise of the Runelords AE; Sandpoint, Light of the Lost Coast

sandpoint.name: The White Deer
sandpoint.description: A refined inn run by a Shoanti man. Caters to a more upscale clientele with fine Shoanti cuisine and quality rooms.
sandpoint.atmosphere: Quiet, refined, Shoanti decor. A large white deer head mounted above the fireplace.

sandpoint.name: The Hagfish
sandpoint.description: A waterfront dive famous for Norah — a large hagfish kept in a murky tank. Patrons who drink the tank water and keep it down get their name carved on the ceiling.
sandpoint.atmosphere: Rowdy, nautical, stinks of fish. The ceiling is covered in carved names. A large slimy tank dominates one corner.
sandpoint.specialFeature: Hagfish Challenge: drink from Norah's tank (DC 12 Fort save). Success earns a prize pot (currently 28 gp) and ceiling fame. Failure: nauseated 1d4 rounds.

sandpoint.name: Fatman's Feedbag
sandpoint.description: A rough dockside tavern popular with laborers and sailors. Cheap drinks and frequent brawls.
sandpoint.atmosphere: Rough, smoky, dangerous. Sawdust on the floor, broken chairs a common sight.

sandpoint.name: The Pixie's Kitten
sandpoint.description: Sandpoint's brothel. A well-kept establishment with a surprisingly good reputation, run with professionalism.
sandpoint.atmosphere: Dimly lit, perfumed, comfortable. Red curtains and plush furnishings.

sandpoint.name: Risa's Place
sandpoint.description: A quiet neighborhood tavern run by the nearly blind Risa. Known for home-cooked meals and a calm atmosphere.
sandpoint.atmosphere: Quiet, homey, the smell of baking bread. A cat sleeps on the bar.

magnimar.name: The Old Fang
magnimar.description: A respectable inn in the Naos district. Popular with merchants and minor nobility visiting the city.
magnimar.atmosphere: Elegant, well-maintained. Paintings of the Irespan on the walls.

magnimar.name: The Shadow Docks Ale House
magnimar.description: A seedy waterfront dive in the Shadow district. Smugglers, thieves, and pirates drink here.
magnimar.atmosphere: Dark, dangerous, stinks of bilge water. Eyes watch you from every corner.

magnimar.name: Bazaar of Sails — Food Stalls
magnimar.description: Dozens of food stalls in the famous open-air market. Cuisine from across Avistan and Garund.
magnimar.atmosphere: Loud, colorful, incredible smells. Exotic spices and foreign tongues everywhere.

turtleback_ferry.name: Bottoms Up Tavern
turtleback_ferry.description: The only tavern in Turtleback Ferry. A warm, simple establishment where fishermen and trappers gather in the evenings.
turtleback_ferry.atmosphere: Rustic, warm, smells of woodsmoke and fish stew. Trophy fish mounted on the walls.
turtleback_ferry.specialFeature: Locals whisper about the 'Paradise' — a gambling barge that recently sank in Claybottom Lake.
turtleback_ferry.source: Rise of the Runelords AE — The Hook Mountain Massacre

squalid.label: Squalid
squalid.description: A filthy hole. Watered-down drinks, vermin, and dangerous patrons.

poor.label: Poor
poor.description: Basic and rough, but functional. Common laborers and travelers.

average.label: Average
average.description: A solid establishment. Clean rooms, decent food, a mix of patrons.

good.label: Good
good.description: Well-appointed with quality service. Merchants, minor nobility, and successful adventurers.

extravagant.label: Extravagant
extravagant.description: Opulent luxury. Nobility, powerful merchants, and visiting dignitaries.

craftsman.label: Craftsman
craftsman.description: Smith, carpenter, weaver, or other artisan

mysterious.label: Mysterious Stranger
mysterious.description: Hooded figure, speaks little, watches everything

tavernEvents.name: Bar Brawl
tavernEvents.description: An insult about someone's lineage sparks a fistfight that quickly spreads across the room.
tavernEvents.successResult: You break up the fight with authority. The crowd respects you — +2 circumstance bonus to Diplomacy checks in this tavern for 1 week.
tavernEvents.failureResult: Chairs fly and tables shatter. You take 1d4 nonlethal damage in the chaos.
tavernEvents.joinDescription: Join the brawl! Nonlethal combat vs 1d3 Commoners (CR 1/2 each). Victory earns bragging rights and 1d6 gp from bets.

tavernEvents.name: Drinking Contest
tavernEvents.description: A boisterous local challenges anyone brave enough to match them drink for drink.
tavernEvents.successResult: You drink them under the table! Prize pot is yours.
tavernEvents.failureResult: The room spins. You're sickened for 1 hour and nauseated for 1d4 rounds.

tavernEvents.name: Bard Performance
tavernEvents.description: A traveling bard takes the stage with tales of heroes and villains.
tavernEvents.successResult: The performance is inspiring. Party members gain a +1 morale bonus to saves vs fear for 24 hours.

tavernEvents.name: Card Game
tavernEvents.description: A group waves you over to join their card game. The stakes look interesting.
tavernEvents.successResult: Your poker face is unbeatable. You clean up at the table.
tavernEvents.failureResult: You lose your stake. The other players smirk as they rake in your coin.

tavernEvents.name: Arm Wrestling Challenge
tavernEvents.description: A burly regular slams their elbow on the table and dares anyone to test their strength.
tavernEvents.successResult: You slam their hand down! The crowd cheers and the prize is yours.
tavernEvents.failureResult: Your arm is forced down. You lose your entry fee and your pride.

tavernEvents.name: Famous Visitor
tavernEvents.description: A well-known figure enters the establishment, drawing whispers and stares.
tavernEvents.successResult: You overhear valuable gossip about current events. +4 to next Gather Information check.

tavernEvents.name: Pickpocket Attempt
tavernEvents.description: A nimble-fingered patron makes a move on your coin purse.
tavernEvents.successResult: You catch the thief's hand in your pocket. They stammer an apology and flee.
tavernEvents.failureResult: You feel lighter. Lose 1d10 gp to the pickpocket.

tavernEvents.name: Mysterious Stranger
tavernEvents.description: A cloaked figure in the corner catches your eye and beckons you over.
tavernEvents.successResult: The stranger shares a cryptic clue about dangers ahead. Gain a campaign-relevant rumor.

tavernEvents.name: Kitchen Fire
tavernEvents.description: Smoke billows from the kitchen as grease catches flame!
tavernEvents.successResult: You help douse the flames quickly. The grateful owner gives you free lodging for the night.
tavernEvents.failureResult: The fire spreads before being contained. The tavern closes for repairs — no services available for 1d4 days.

tavernEvents.name: Admiring Glances
tavernEvents.description: An attractive patron seems very interested in your tales of adventure.
tavernEvents.successResult: A pleasant evening. +2 morale bonus to Charisma-based checks for 24 hours.
tavernEvents.failureResult: Things get awkward. No mechanical effect, but the GM gets to describe the embarrassment.

tavernEvents.name: Drunken Prophecy
tavernEvents.description: An old drunk grabs your arm and delivers a rambling prophecy with wild eyes.
tavernEvents.successResult: The words seem like nonsense, but something rings true. Gain a cryptic campaign hint.

tavernEvents.name: Bad Batch
tavernEvents.description: Something in the food doesn't agree with you.
tavernEvents.successResult: Your constitution holds. Just a brief rumble in the gut.
tavernEvents.failureResult: You're sickened for 1d4 hours. -2 to attack rolls, saves, skill checks, and ability checks.

intoxicationLevels.effects: +2 alchemical bonus to Cha, -2 to Dex and Wis
+2 alchemical bonus to Str and Cha, -4 to Dex and Wis, -2 to Int
-6 Dex, -4 Wis, -4 Int, staggered condition, speak only in slurred words
Fall unconscious for 1d4 hours. DC 25 Fort or become nauseated upon waking for 1 hour.

dwarf.note: +2 racial bonus to Fort saves vs poison (applies to alcohol)

drinkingRules.hangoverEffects: Sickened condition (-2 attacks, saves, skills, ability checks) until hangover passes

restAndRecovery.baseHealing: 1 HP per character level per 8 hours of rest
restAndRecovery.longTermCare: 2 HP per level with Heal check DC 15 (long-term care)

wilderness.description: Sleeping rough — Fort DC 15 or fatigued next day

poor.description: Uncomfortable but sheltered — Fort DC 12 or fatigued

common.description: A proper bed — normal rest and healing

good.description: Comfortable room — enhanced rest, +50% overnight healing

suite.description: Luxurious rest — double overnight healing, +1 morale bonus for 4 hours

1.rumor: Goblins have been getting bolder lately. Raids on outlying farms have increased.
The new cathedral is finally finished. Father Zantus is planning a grand consecration at the Swallowtail Festival.
Lonjiku Kaijitsu's been in a foul mood ever since his daughter refused to give up that tavern of hers.
They say the Late Unpleasantness — the murders and the fire — were connected somehow. Dark magic, perhaps.
The five goblin tribes have never worked together before. Something — or someone — must be uniting them.
Father Tobyn's daughter, Nualia, died in the chapel fire... but some say her body was never found.
There are ancient Thassilonian tunnels beneath the town. Old Brodert Quink insists the Old Light was a weapon, not a lighthouse.
The old smugglers' tunnels under town connect to the Glassworks somehow.
A half-elf was spotted sneaking around the Glassworks at night recently.
The Rusty Dragon's owner, Ameiko, used to be an adventurer. She still keeps a sword behind the bar.
A noble from Magnimar has been visiting frequently. They say he's sweet on one of the heroes of the goblin raid.
The Sandpoint Devil isn't just a legend. Something terrible lives out in the farmlands.

2.rumor: People have been disappearing around the farmlands south of town. The scarecrows seem... wrong.
Sheriff Hemlock has gone to Magnimar to request additional soldiers. Something has him spooked.
Farmer Grump swears the scarecrows in Hambley's field are walking at night.
There's been a series of murders in Magnimar too. The killer leaves a seven-pointed star carved into victims.
Foxglove Manor — 'The Misgivings' — is cursed. Nobody goes near the place anymore.
The Foxglove family has a dark history. Aldern's parents died under mysterious circumstances.
The murders are connected to an ancient cult. The seven-pointed star is a Thassilonian symbol — the Sihedron Rune.
That nice nobleman Foxglove hasn't been seen around town lately. Hope nothing happened to him.

3.rumor: Fort Rannick has gone silent. The Black Arrows haven't reported in weeks.
Ogres have been moving down from Hook Mountain in greater numbers than anyone's seen.
The Paradise gambling barge sank in Claybottom Lake, but some say it was no accident.
The Graul family in the Kreegwood are inbred ogrekin. They've been preying on travelers for years.
Skull's Crossing — the ancient dam — is cracking. If it breaks, it'll flood the entire valley.

4.rumor: Giant raids are increasing along the Storval Plateau. Stone giants have been spotted.
An ancient fortress called Jorgenfist lies deep in the Iron Peaks. The giants are gathering there.
The stone giant leader, Mokmurian, is no ordinary giant. They say he wields ancient Thassilonian magic.
The Runelords are returning. The ancient mage-tyrants who once ruled all of Varisia.

1.rumor: The Council of Ushers is arguing about taxes again. Lord-Mayor Grobaras doesn't care about anything but his dinner.
There's been trouble in the little town of Sandpoint up the coast. Goblins attacked during their festival.
The Justice Court has been cracking down on Sczarni operations in the Shadow district.
Something's not right at the Seven's Sawmill. Strange noises at odd hours.

2.rumor: A series of murders has the city guard on edge. They call the killer 'The Skinsaw Man.'
The victims all seem connected to the Foxglove family somehow.
The murders are tied to a secret society. The Skinsaw Cult operates out of a sawmill.
Justice Ironbriar isn't what he seems. The elven judge has been acting strangely.
The cult worships Father Skinsaw — an aspect of Norgorber, god of murder.

3.rumor: Fort Rannick has been silent for weeks. We used to see Black Arrows passing through regularly.
The Paradise sank in a storm last month. Good riddance — that gambling barge was nothing but trouble.
The Graul farmstead in the Kreegwood is a place best avoided. The family there aren't... right.
The floodwaters have been rising steadily. Old-timers say the dam on the Storval Rise is failing.
Lucrecia, the woman who ran the Paradise, was involved in dark magic. She had tattoos that moved.
The ogres of Hook Mountain are organized under a new leader. They call themselves the Kreeg.
